ocr.text: In December 2023, McKinsey & Company reported Revenue per consultant across various Consultant Types in the Professional
& Business Services sector in INR, revealing that Senior Consultants generated a Revenue of $500M with a Year-over-Year
Change of 10%, while Junior Consultants contributed $300M with a Year-over-Year Change of 15%. The Total Revenue
amounted to $800M, reflecting a 12% increase, constituting a Market Share of 25% in Consulting, with a Research segment
accounting for 15% of the Total Market. The overall Revenue per Consultant was reported as $1,000M, indicating a Growth
Rate of 12%. The Average Revenue per Consultant stood at $50M, with Senior Growth at 8% and Junior Growth at 9%.
ConsultantType  Revenue(INR)  YoYChange(%)
SeniorConsultant  500M  10%
JuniorConsultant  300M  15%
TotalRevenue  800M  12%
MarketShare(%)  Consulting  25%
Research  15%  TotalMarket
1000M  12%  RevenueperConsultant
Average  50M  GrowthRate(%)
Overall  8%  SeniorGrowth
9%  JuniorGrowth  7%
ConsultantHeadcount  Total  16
Senior  10  Junior
The total Consultant Headcount reached 16, comprising 10 Senior Consultants and 6 Junior Consultants. The detailed financial
metrics exhibit the firm’s strong positioning within the market, demonstrating resilience and adaptability in a competitive
landscape. This analysis underscores McKinsey's commitment to enhancing its consulting practice while maintaining a robust
workforce that meets the evolving demands of the market. Overall, the figures reflect a strategic focus on growth and market
share expansion, positioning McKinsey for sustained success in the Asia-Pacific region. With a clear emphasis on both Senior
and Junior staffing levels, the company is poised to leverage its expertise across various consulting domains, ensuring continued
relevance and profitability in a dynamic business environment.
